name: Tofu Satay with Tangy Dipping Sauce
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Firm or extra firm tofu
3 tbsp White or red wine vinegar
recipeInstructions:
Drain the tofu and place the cakes between several layers of paper towels. Weight the top with a heavy plate and leave for 1 to 3 hours.
Prepare the Satay Sauce and Dipping Sauce while draining.
SATAY SAUCE: Heat oil in a pot over medium-high heat. Cook onion and garlic until the onion is wilting, approximately 2 minutes. Add curry powder and cook for 30 seconds. Add coconut milk, soy sauce, sugar, red chili powder, lemongrass, and peanut butter. Cook over very low heat, stirring constantly, for 5 minutes.
DIPPING SAUCE: In a small pot, over low heat, dissolve sugar in the water. Remove from the heat and stir in vinegar, soy sauce, and dried chili flakes. When completely cool, add carrot and peanuts; serve with sauces.
When the tofu has drained, slice each cake lengthways into 4 strips. Combine oil, lemongrass, garlic, curry powder, chili pepper, and sugar in a food processor and process until smooth. Pour this mixture over the tofu strips, gently toss to coat the pieces, and marinate for an hour at room temperature.
Soak 16 wooden skewers in water for 15 minutes. Carefully insert two skewers parallel and about 1 inch apart into each tofu slice. Oil the grill. On a closed grill over medium-hot coals, grill the tofu, turning once, until browned, 10 to 12 minutes. Serve with Satay Sauce and Dipping Sauce.
